page: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor with an x86_64 architecture, this configuration delivers 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. The instance includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth metrics fall within the average range. For instance, the system achieves a passmark CPU score of 18127.64 and a memory score of 2641.22. Large block uncached memory bandwidth registers at 117.03 GB/sec for reads and 77.42 GB/sec for writes.

Tradeoffs are evident in workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources or heavy parallel processing. While single-threaded tasks perform well, the shared CPU allocation may introduce variability under sustained multi-core loads. Additionally, LLM inference benchmarks show average performance for small 135M models but drop to the bottom 25% for medium 7B model prompt processing, registering at 18.38 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model combined with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a practical resource density for non-production environments, staging, and general-purpose workloads. It is appropriate for static web serving, which benchmarks at 8.45 GB/sec, Redis operations at 15.29 million operations per second, and moderate database operations.
description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 8 shared vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, it features 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and Redis deployments, though the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained high-compute tasks. It delivers balanced resource density for cost-conscious development and staging environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 160 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.

tagline: A shared-CPU instance featuring AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory density, and local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
Shared CPU allocation suitable for web serving and Redis deployments
